bids.reasoning: Since this is my first bid at t=0.0, I will target my maximum possible utility. The opponent's message claims their opening offer is a balanced win-win, but the utility their offer provides me is minimal, confirming it as standard cheap talk. I am responding with my top preferences across the board to establish a strong anchor.

The current time is very early (t=0.02). The opponent claims to have made a concession on issueD, but their previous offer for issueD (valueE) gave me slightly more utility than their current offer (valueD). Because their offers currently yield extremely low utility for me (around 0.23 based on my weights), I will maintain a high-utility posture. I will make a very minor concession on my lowest-impact issue, issueE, moving from valueB to valueA, to signal a willingness to explore the space while protecting my core interests.

The opponent's offers continue to yield very low utility for me despite their "win-win" messaging, as they are making only marginal changes on my lower-weighted issues. The current time, t=0.1, is very early in the negotiation.
